title: Hook Norton, Wiggington & Sibford (late August 1990)

main_route.excerpt: We went to a pub campsite - blah! very crowded & a pond, near Hook Norton David arrived after dark. A pretty £ pub.
we set off early to find a new campsite. We were very lucky to find one not noted in the camping magazine, at a wildfowl trust. Wiggington
Monday We moved to Sibford for lunch + in the afternoon we walked through villages, passing the school.
On the way home we saw a nice farm campsite near Compton Wynyates.

stops.normalized_name: St Giles' Church, Wigginton, Oxfordshire, UK
stops.excerpt: On the Sunday we walked to Wiggington, to go to church, but there was only an evensong. 2 stone recumbent effigies in the chancel, odd spiral steps up at the crossing, + lovely coloured stone.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page A_0031_R may share content with a preceding trip
mixed_end: page A_0032_L may share content with a following trip
summary: A late August 1990 camping trip to the north Oxfordshire/south Warwickshire countryside, starting at a crowded pub campsite near Hook Norton before moving to a much-enjoyed wildfowl trust campsite at Wigginton for the weekend. The family explored the local villages, attended an evensong, enjoyed a barbecue, and on the Monday drove through Sibford for lunch before heading home to Birmingham.
extraction_notes: August 29 1990 was a Wednesday; the diary describes at least three nights (Wednesday at Hook Norton, Saturday and Sunday at Wigginton, with Monday as the return day via Sibford). Compton Wynyates is mentioned only as a farm campsite spotted 'on the way home' — not visited — so it is not included as a stop. 'David' arrived at Hook Norton after dark; identity unclear (possibly a friend/relative of the family). Sibford is most likely Sibford Gower or Sibford Ferris; geocoded as Sibford Gower, the larger of the two villages. Pages are marked mixed_start and mixed_end in the source, indicating content may bleed into adjacent trips.
